changed_functions.name: AfdAddConnectedReference
key_changes: Patched adds an EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071) check; when enabled, the connected-reference counter update is made atomic with respect to the lock
When the feature is disabled, the original behavior is preserved
Part of the in-flight synchronization mechanism drained by AfdTLListenComplete
changed_functions.security_notes: Part of the feature-gated synchronization change (Finding 1). Gated by g_Feature_3289849145_60513071; original behavior retained when disabled.

changed_functions.name: AfdConnect
key_changes: Connect/accept dispatch function; changes are primarily offset shifts due to the new endpoint field at +0xf8
Calls to AfdCreateConnection updated to the patched address
Several structure field accesses shifted by +8 bytes

changed_functions.name: AfdTLListenComplete
key_changes: PATCHED: after overwriting [rbx+0x10]/[rbx+0x18], adds a spin-wait 'while (*(rbx+0xf8)) _mm_pause()' before invoking the old function pointer, gated by EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071)
UNPATCHED: overwrites [rbx+0x10] (context) and [rbx+0x18] (function pointer) then immediately invokes the old function pointer via the CFG guard dispatch, without draining in-flight operations
The racy code is in the success path (status >= 0); the status < 0 error path takes a spinlock and is unchanged in substance
Endpoint field inserted at +0xf8 shifts later offsets (e.g. 0x158 -> 0x160)
changed_functions.security_notes: RACE CONDITION (Finding 1), feature-gated: the unpatched AfdTLListenComplete swaps the endpoint dispatch function pointer ([rbx+0x18]) and context ([rbx+0x10]) and invokes the old pointer without draining concurrent TDI I/O-control operations. The patch adds a per-endpoint in-flight counter at [rbx+0xf8], maintained by the endpoint dispatch helpers and spin-waited on here after the swap and before the old-pointer invocation. Both the counter maintenance and the wait are gated by g_Feature_3289849145_60513071 with the original no-wait behavior retained when disabled.
changed_functions.assembly_diff:
UNPATCHED AfdTLListenComplete @ 0x1C00597D0 (success path):
00000001C005982A  mov     rax, [rbx+18h]                 ; old function pointer
00000001C005982E  lea     rdx, AfdTLDontCareIOCompletion
00000001C0059835  mov     rcx, [rbx+10h]                 ; old context
00000001C0059843  mov     [rbx+10h], r15                 ; overwrite context (no wait)
00000001C0059847  mov     [rbx+18h], r14                 ; overwrite function ptr (no wait)
00000001C0059850  mov     rax, [rax]
00000001C0059853  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; CFG-guarded indirect call

PATCHED AfdTLListenComplete @ 0x1C005A960 (success path):
00000001C005A9BA  mov     r12, [rbx+10h]                 ; old context
00000001C005A9BE  lea     rcx, g_Feature_3289849145_60513071_FeatureDescriptorDetails
00000001C005A9C5  mov     r13, [rbx+18h]                 ; old function pointer
00000001C005A9D5  mov     [rbx+10h], r15
00000001C005A9D9  mov     [rbx+18h], r14
00000001C005A9DD  call    EvaluateCurrentState
00000001C005A9E2  test    eax, eax
00000001C005A9E4  jz      short loc_1C005A9F6            ; feature off -> skip wait
00000001C005A9E8  pause
00000001C005A9EA  mov     rax, [rbx+0F8h]                ; in-flight counter
00000001C005A9F1  test    rax, rax
00000001C005A9F4  jnz     short loc_1C005A9E8            ; spin until drained
00000001C005AA0C  mov     rax, [r13+0]
00000001C005AA13  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

changed_functions.name: AfdTLConnectEventHandler
key_changes: Large function with mostly offset shifts and callee address changes
Structure accesses shifted by +8 in patched version due to the new +0xf8 field

changed_functions.name: AfdSetInformation
key_changes: Large function with offset shifts and callee address changes
No new security checks added

changed_functions.name: AfdTLDelayAcceptListenComplete
key_changes: Dispatcher: updated callee addresses to the relocated patched targets

changed_functions.name: AfdTLPauseUnPause
key_changes: PATCHED: adds EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071) gate; when enabled, increments/decrements the endpoint in-flight counter at +0xf8 around the TDI dispatch call
UNPATCHED: performs the TDI dispatch without any in-flight counter
Structure offsets shifted by +8 bytes due to the new +0xf8 field
changed_functions.security_notes: Part of the feature-gated synchronization change (Finding 1). Increments the endpoint in-flight counter drained by AfdTLListenComplete.

changed_functions.name: AfdQueryCompartmentId
key_changes: PATCHED: adds EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071) gate with in-flight counter increment/decrement at endpoint +0xf8 around the TDI dispatch call
UNPATCHED: performs the TDI dispatch without an in-flight counter
changed_functions.security_notes: Part of the feature-gated synchronization change (Finding 1). The counter at endpoint +0xf8 is the same one drained in AfdTLListenComplete.

changed_functions.name: AfdDoDatagramConnect
key_changes: PATCHED: when EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_566223161_61092118) is enabled, caches (BYTE1(*(rbx+8)) & 1) (bit 0x100) BEFORE ObfDereferenceObject(a1) and uses the cached value for the ExFreePoolWithTag decision
UNPATCHED: reads *(rbx+8) & 0x100 AFTER ObfDereferenceObject(a1)
When the feature is disabled, the patched build retains the original post-dereference read
Structure offsets shifted by +8 bytes
changed_functions.security_notes: Feature-gated defensive lifetime hardening (Finding 2): in the unpatched cleanup path, ObfDereferenceObject(a1) is called then *(rbx+8) is read to decide whether to free MasterIrp, where rbx = *(a1+0x18). If the dereference frees the allocation backing rbx, the read would touch freed memory. Whether the dereference actually frees that allocation is not demonstrable from these binaries. Gated by g_Feature_566223161_61092118 with the original read retained when disabled.

changed_functions.name: AfdCreateConnection
key_changes: VALIDATION FIX: patched REMOVES the EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_1484999993_59735560) gate that wrapped the device-consistency validation

UNPATCHED: when that feature is disabled, the ObReferenceObjectByHandle + IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison is skipped, allowing a handle from a different device to be associated with the AFD connection
Patched always performs the device-consistency validation whenever a handle is supplied
changed_functions.security_notes: Device-consistency validation made unconditional (Finding 3). The unpatched build gates the check that the supplied handle's device stack matches the connection's transport device behind g_Feature_1484999993_59735560; when disabled the check is skipped. The patched build removes the gate. The gate symbol is absent from the patched binary.

changed_functions.name: AfdCreateConnection_Old
key_changes: Same gate removal as AfdCreateConnection: the EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_1484999993_59735560) wrapper around the device-consistency validation is removed
Patched version always performs ObReferenceObjectByHandle + IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison
changed_functions.security_notes: Same device-consistency validation change as AfdCreateConnection (Finding 3).

changed_functions.name: AfdCleanupCore
key_changes: Endpoint cleanup/teardown; changes are offset shifts (+8 bytes) and callee address updates
Accesses shifted from the 0x158 to the 0x160 region consistent with the new +0xf8 field

changed_functions.name: AfdGetInformation
key_changes: Offset shifts and callee address changes throughout
No new security checks added

changed_functions.name: AfdGetAddress
key_changes: PATCHED: adds EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071) gate with in-flight counter increment/decrement at endpoint +0xf8 around the TDI dispatch call in the recv paths
UNPATCHED: performs the TDI dispatch without an in-flight counter
changed_functions.security_notes: Part of the feature-gated synchronization change (Finding 1). Increments the endpoint in-flight counter drained by AfdTLListenComplete.

security_findings.delivery: feature-gated (staged) behind g_Feature_3289849145_60513071; original no-wait behavior retained when disabled
security_findings.entry_point: AFD IOCTL dispatch via NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd (TDI listen-completion path)
call_chain: TDI listen completion -> AfdTLListenComplete (success path, status >= 0)

In-flight counter maintained by endpoint dispatch helpers AfdGetAddress (0x1C0035A00), AfdQueryCompartmentId (0x1C00357D0), AfdTLPauseUnPause (0x1C0058550) and AfdAddConnectedReference (0x1C0047C0C), reachable from AFD IOCTL dispatch
security_findings.vulnerability_class: race condition / TOCTOU on endpoint dispatch-pointer swap
security_findings.description: In the success path (IRP status >= 0), the unpatched AfdTLListenComplete loads the endpoint's dispatch context ([rbx+0x10]) and function pointer ([rbx+0x18]), overwrites both with the new values, and immediately invokes the old function pointer through the CFG guard dispatch, without draining TDI I/O-control operations that may be in flight on the same endpoint. The patch adds a per-endpoint in-flight counter at [rbx+0xf8], incremented/decremented by the endpoint dispatch helpers, and makes AfdTLListenComplete spin-wait for it to reach zero after the swap and before the old-pointer invocation. The counter maintenance and the wait are gated by EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071); when the feature is disabled the original no-wait behavior is retained.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) Create an AFD endpoint via \Device\Afd. 2) Issue TDI I/O-control operations reaching the endpoint dispatch helpers so the in-flight counter at endpoint +0xf8 is non-zero (only meaningful when g_Feature_3289849145_60513071 is enabled). 3) Concurrently drive a TDI listen completion so AfdTLListenComplete runs with status >= 0, swapping the endpoint dispatch pointers. Without the drain, the old function pointer is invoked while an in-flight dispatch is still running on the same endpoint.
security_findings.primitive: Kernel data race on the endpoint dispatch fields ([rbx+0x10]/[rbx+0x18]) with respect to concurrent TDI operations. The indirect call is CFG-guarded (__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr). No exploitation primitive beyond a potential race-induced fault is established from these binaries; the fix is feature-gated.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED AfdTLListenComplete @ 0x1C00597D0 (success path):
00000001C005982A  mov     rax, [rbx+18h]                 ; old function pointer
00000001C005982E  lea     rdx, AfdTLDontCareIOCompletion
00000001C0059835  mov     rcx, [rbx+10h]                 ; old context
00000001C0059843  mov     [rbx+10h], r15                 ; overwrite context (no wait)
00000001C0059847  mov     [rbx+18h], r14                 ; overwrite function ptr (no wait)
00000001C0059850  mov     rax, [rax]
00000001C0059853  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr ; CFG-guarded indirect call

PATCHED AfdTLListenComplete @ 0x1C005A960 (success path):
00000001C005A9BA  mov     r12, [rbx+10h]                 ; old context
00000001C005A9BE  lea     rcx, g_Feature_3289849145_60513071_FeatureDescriptorDetails
00000001C005A9C5  mov     r13, [rbx+18h]                 ; old function pointer
00000001C005A9D5  mov     [rbx+10h], r15
00000001C005A9D9  mov     [rbx+18h], r14
00000001C005A9DD  call    EvaluateCurrentState
00000001C005A9E2  test    eax, eax
00000001C005A9E4  jz      short loc_1C005A9F6            ; feature off -> skip wait
00000001C005A9E8  pause
00000001C005A9EA  mov     rax, [rbx+0F8h]                ; in-flight counter
00000001C005A9F1  test    rax, rax
00000001C005A9F4  jnz     short loc_1C005A9E8            ; spin until drained
00000001C005AA0C  mov     rax, [r13+0]
00000001C005AA13  call    cs:__guard_dispatch_icall_fptr

breakpoints.why: Entry. rbx (set at 0x1C005980C from [r10+0x18]) is the endpoint. ebp is the IRP status; must be >= 0 for the success path. Inspect [rbx+0x10]/[rbx+0x18] before and after the swap at 0x1C0059843/0x1C0059847.
Loads the old function pointer from [rbx+0x18]; start of the unsynchronized region.
The CFG-guarded indirect call through the old function pointer; end of the unsynchronized region.

watch: rbx+0x10 = endpoint dispatch context (overwritten at 0x1C0059843 with r15)
rbx+0x18 = endpoint dispatch function pointer (overwritten at 0x1C0059847 with r14)
rbx+0xf8 = in-flight counter (added in patch; spin-waited before the old-pointer invocation)
ebp = IRP status (must be >= 0 to take the success path)
key_offsets: 0x1C0059824: 'js' to the error path when status < 0; the race is in the fall-through success path
0x1C0059843/0x1C0059847: overwrite context/function pointer with no drain
PATCHED equivalent adds 'while (*(rbx+0xf8)) pause' before the call, gated by EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_3289849145_60513071)
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1) Open \Device\Afd and create an AFD endpoint. 2) Issue TDI I/O-control operations reaching the endpoint dispatch helpers so the +0xf8 counter is non-zero. 3) From another thread, drive a TDI listen completion so AfdTLListenComplete runs with status >= 0.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: In the unpatched build the old function pointer is invoked without draining in-flight endpoint operations; the change adds that drain when the feature is enabled.
debugging_aids.notes: Endpoint fields: +0x10 = dispatch context, +0x18 = dispatch function pointer, +0x30 = spinlock (error path), +0x158/+0x160 = operation state word, +0xf8 = in-flight counter (added in patch). The counter and the wait are gated by g_Feature_3289849145_60513071.

security_findings.delivery: feature-gated behind g_Feature_566223161_61092118; original post-dereference read retained when disabled
security_findings.entry_point: AFD datagram connect dispatch via NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd
call_chain: AFD connect IOCTL dispatch -> AfdDoDatagramConnect cleanup/error path (LABEL_42)
security_findings.vulnerability_class: TOCTOU / potential use-after-free on a state flag read after ObfDereferenceObject (defensive hardening)
security_findings.description: In the unpatched AfdDoDatagramConnect cleanup path (LABEL_42), ObfDereferenceObject(a1) is called and then *(v5+8) is read (bit 0x100) to decide whether to free MasterIrp with the 'AfdI' pool tag, where v5 = *(a1+0x18). If dropping the reference on a1 were to free the allocation backing v5, the read would touch freed memory. The patched build, when g_Feature_566223161_61092118 is enabled, caches bit 0x100 of *(v5+8) into a byte before the dereference and uses the cached value; when disabled it retains the original post-dereference read. Whether the dereference actually frees the memory backing v5 is not demonstrable from these binaries, so this is characterized as a defensive lifetime hardening rather than a proven reachable use-after-free.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) Create an AFD socket and drive a datagram connect that reaches the AfdDoDatagramConnect cleanup path (LABEL_42). 2) The path calls ObfDereferenceObject(a1) and, when the feature is disabled, reads *(v5+8) to decide whether to free MasterIrp.
security_findings.primitive: If the memory backing v5 were freed by the dereference, the flag read would return stale data affecting the MasterIrp free decision. No reachable memory-corruption primitive is established from these binaries.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED AfdDoDatagramConnect LABEL_42 @ 0x1C0064510:
00000001C0064510  call    cs:__imp_ObfDereferenceObject
00000001C0064516  test    dword ptr [rbx+8], 100h        ; read after deref
00000001C006451D  jnz     short loc_1C0064532
00000001C006451F  mov     edx, 49646641h                 ; 'AfdI'
00000001C0064524  mov     rcx, r15                       ; MasterIrp
00000001C0064527  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag

PATCHED AfdDoDatagramConnect LABEL_42 @ 0x1C005A719:
00000001C005A719  call    EvaluateCurrentState
00000001C005A720  jz      short loc_1C005A72E
00000001C005A722  mov     r15d, [rbx+8]                  ; cache before deref
00000001C005A726  shr     r15d, 8
00000001C005A72A  and     r15b, 1                        ; bit 0x100
00000001C005A732  call    cs:__imp_ObfDereferenceObject
00000001C005A73F  call    EvaluateCurrentState
00000001C005A748  test    r15b, r15b                     ; use cached value
00000001C005A74D  test    dword ptr [rbx+8], 100h        ; else retained old read
00000001C005A756  mov     edx, 49646641h                 ; 'AfdI'
00000001C005A75E  call    cs:__imp_ExFreePoolWithTag

breakpoints.why: Entry. v5/rbx = *(a1+0x18). Track a1's reference count around ObfDereferenceObject in the LABEL_42 cleanup path and whether the memory backing rbx remains valid after the deref.
The ObfDereferenceObject call; the following instruction at 0x1C0064516 reads *(rbx+8).

watch: rbx = *(a1+0x18); read at 0x1C0064516 after the dereference
rbx+8 = state flags; bit 0x100 controls the MasterIrp free decision
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1) Create an AFD socket. 2) Drive a datagram connect reaching AfdDoDatagramConnect. 3) Force the LABEL_42 cleanup path.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: Unpatched reads the flag after the dereference; patched caches it before, when the feature is enabled.
debugging_aids.notes: rbx is derived from a1 at function start (rbx = *(a1+0x18)). Whether a1's dereference frees the allocation backing rbx is not demonstrable from these binaries.

security_findings.entry_point: AFD connect IOCTL via NtDeviceIoControlFile on \Device\Afd
call_chain: AFD connect IOCTL dispatch -> AfdCreateConnection (and AfdCreateConnection_Old @ 0x1C0015AF8)
security_findings.description: In the unpatched AfdCreateConnection, when a file handle is supplied, the device-consistency validation (ObReferenceObjectByHandle on the handle, then IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef comparison against the connection's transport device, returning STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER on mismatch) is wrapped in EvaluateCurrentState(&g_Feature_1484999993_59735560). When the feature is disabled, the whole validation block is skipped and a handle from a different device stack can be associated with the AFD connection. The patched build removes the gate and always performs the validation when a handle is supplied. The gate symbol g_Feature_1484999993_59735560 appears in the unpatched decompilation of both AfdCreateConnection and AfdCreateConnection_Old and is absent from the patched build.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: 1) Open \Device\Afd and create a socket. 2) Prepare a file handle whose device stack differs from the AFD transport device. 3) Issue the AFD connect IOCTL supplying that handle. 4) On unpatched systems where g_Feature_1484999993_59735560 is disabled, the device-consistency check is skipped and the handle is accepted; the patched build returns STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER.
security_findings.primitive: Logic/validation gap allowing a mismatched-device handle to be associated with an AFD connection. Downstream type-confusion consequences are not demonstrated from these binaries.
security_findings.assembly:
UNPATCHED AfdCreateConnection gate @ 0x1C00494AD:
00000001C00494A6  lea     rcx, g_Feature_1484999993_59735560_FeatureDescriptorDetails
00000001C00494AD  call    EvaluateCurrentState
00000001C00494B4  jz      loc_1C0049575                  ; feature off -> skip validation
00000001C00494D3  call    cs:__imp_ObReferenceObjectByHandle
00000001C0049503  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef

PATCHED AfdCreateConnection @ 0x1C0049240 performs the validation with no EvaluateCurrentState gate:
00000001C0049510  call    cs:__imp_ObReferenceObjectByHandle
00000001C0049567  call    cs:__imp_IoGetDeviceAttachmentBaseRef

breakpoints.why: The EvaluateCurrentState gate. When it returns 0, the jz at 0x1C00494B4 skips ObReferenceObjectByHandle and the device-match comparison.

watch: supplied file handle to associate with the connection
return value of EvaluateCurrentState at 0x1C00494AD - if 0, validation is skipped
key_offsets: 0x1C00494AD: 'call EvaluateCurrentState' followed by 'jz loc_1C0049575' - the skip in the unpatched build
In patched AfdCreateConnection this gate is removed
debugging_aids.trigger_setup: 1) Open \Device\Afd, create a socket. 2) Prepare a file handle from a different device. 3) Issue the AFD connect IOCTL passing the mismatched handle.
debugging_aids.expected_observation: On unpatched systems with the feature disabled, the handle is accepted without device validation; on patched systems the device mismatch yields STATUS_INVALID_PARAMETER.
debugging_aids.notes: The same gate removal is present in AfdCreateConnection_Old (0x1C0015AF8).

summary: The patch introduces a synchronization (drain-wait) mechanism around the endpoint dispatch-pointer swap in the TDI listen-completion callback AfdTLListenComplete (unpatched 0x1C00597D0, patched 0x1C005A960), addressing a race condition (CWE-362). The unpatched success path swaps the endpoint dispatch context ([rbx+0x10]) and function pointer ([rbx+0x18]) and invokes the old pointer via the CFG guard dispatch without draining concurrent TDI operations; the patch adds a per-endpoint in-flight counter at [rbx+0xf8], maintained by the dispatch helpers (AfdGetAddress, AfdQueryCompartmentId, AfdTLPauseUnPause, AfdAddConnectedReference) and spin-waited on before the old-pointer invocation, all gated by g_Feature_3289849145_60513071 with the original behavior retained when disabled. The patch also caches a state flag before ObfDereferenceObject in the AfdDoDatagramConnect cleanup path (gated by g_Feature_566223161_61092118; a defensive lifetime hardening whose reachability as a use-after-free is not demonstrable from these binaries), and removes the g_Feature_1484999993_59735560 gate around the device-consistency validation in AfdCreateConnection and AfdCreateConnection_Old, making that validation unconditional.
